name: Inner Mongolia Autonomous Region (内蒙古自治区 / ᠥᠪᠥᠷ ᠮᠣᠩᠭᠣᠯ ᠤᠨ ᠥᠪᠡᠷᠲᠡᠭᠡᠨ ᠵᠠᠰᠠᠬᠤ ᠣᠷᠣᠨ)
summary: The Inner Mongolia Autonomous Region (内蒙古自治区 Nèi Měnggǔ Zìzhìqū in Chinese / ᠥᠪᠥᠷ ᠮᠣᠩᠭᠣᠯ ᠤᠨ ᠥᠪᠡᠷᠲᠡᠭᠡᠨ ᠵᠠᠰᠠᠬᠤ ᠣᠷᠣᠨ Öbör Mongγol-un öbertegen Jasaqu Orun in Traditional Mongolian Script) is a Northern Chinese civil-law Chinese autonomous-region constituent unit of the People's Republic of China — structurally distinctive globally as the oldest Chinese autonomous-region in modern Chinese state formation history (Inner Mongolia Autonomous Government established 1 May 1947 — preceding the founding of the People's Republic of China by 2 years 5 months 1 October 1949, making Inner Mongolia the precedent template for all subsequent Chinese autonomous-region frameworks), as the third-largest Chinese province-level division by area (~1,183,000 km² — ~12.3% of Chinese land area, third after Xinjiang + Tibet), as the only modern Chinese sub-national territory operating Traditional Mongolian Script ᠮᠣᠩᠭᠣᠯ ᠪᠢᠴᠢᠭ in formal government usage (Inner Mongolia is the only modern jurisdiction globally where Traditional Mongolian Script — the vertical script descended from Old Uyghur framework — is used in official government documentation, as Mongolia itself uses Cyrillic-script Mongolian since 1946 Mongolian script reform), and as the central jurisdiction of the 2020 Inner Mongolian-language education protests (August-September 2020 protests against Chinese Government policy reducing Mongolian-language instruction in Inner Mongolian schools — pivotal cultural-political-identity contestation framework). Inner Mongolia historically operated under multiple Mongol Khanate frameworks (Mongol Empire 1206-1368 + Northern Yuan 1368-1635 + Qing Inner Mongolian league framework 1635-1912), was contested 1912-1947 between Republic of China + Mongolian People's Republic (1924-1992) + various Inner Mongolian self-government frameworks, established Inner Mongolia Autonomous Government 1 May 1947, and was formally incorporated into People's Republic of China 1949. Inner Mongolia Autonomous Region comprises ~1,183,000 km² with a population of ~24 million (~4.2 million Mongolian + ~19 million Han + others). Family-law framework operates under the federal Chinese civil-law framework (Chinese Marriage Law 1980 + Chinese Civil Code 2020 Family Law portion Articles 1040-1118) applied uniformly across Chinese autonomous regions. The Inner Mongolia High People's Court is the apex regional court; final appellate jurisdiction lies with the Supreme People's Court of China. Inner Mongolia is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label. China is a Hague Convention 1980 signatory but not a party — Inner Mongolia is governed by the Chinese non-ratification status.

statutory_framework.citation: Constitution of the People's Republic of China Article 4 (Regional Ethnic Autonomy)
statutory_framework.title: Constitution of China Article 4
statutory_framework.relevance: Chinese Constitutional Article 4 providing for Regional Ethnic Autonomy framework — applicable to Inner Mongolia Autonomous Region.

statutory_framework.title: Regional Ethnic Autonomy Law
statutory_framework.relevance: Chinese Federal Law of 31 May 1984 (revised 28 February 2001) establishing Regional Ethnic Autonomy framework — applicable to Inner Mongolia Autonomous Region.

statutory_framework.citation: Inner Mongolia Autonomous Government Establishment Declaration 1947
statutory_framework.title: Inner Mongolia Autonomous Government 1947
statutory_framework.relevance: Establishment Declaration of 1 May 1947 — preceding the founding of the People's Republic of China by 2 years 5 months, making Inner Mongolia the precedent template for all subsequent Chinese autonomous-region frameworks.

statutory_framework.title: Inner Mongolia Mongolian-Language Education Regulations
statutory_framework.relevance: Inner Mongolia Autonomous Region regulations of August 2020 reducing Mongolian-language instruction in Inner Mongolian schools — catalyst for 2020 Inner Mongolian-language education protests.

statutory_framework.citation: Chinese Civil Code 2020 Family Law portion (Articles 1040-1118)
statutory_framework.title: Chinese Civil Code Family Law
statutory_framework.relevance: Chinese Federal Civil Code 2020 Family Law portion applicable uniformly across Inner Mongolia Autonomous Region.

apex_courts.name: Inner Mongolia High People's Court
apex_courts.role: Apex regional appellate court for civil and criminal matters from Inner Mongolia Autonomous Region.

apex_courts.name: Supreme People's Court of China
apex_courts.role: Final appellate court for civil and criminal matters from Inner Mongolia Autonomous Region.

professional_regulators.name: Chinese National Health Commission (国家卫生健康委员会)
professional_regulators.role: Chinese Federal professional regulatory framework applicable in Inner Mongolia Autonomous Region.

anonymisation_convention: Inner Mongolia family-court decisions are anonymised per Chinese court practice using initials.

key_developments.title: Mongol Empire founded
key_developments.description: Mongol Empire founded 1206 by Chinggis Khan at Onon River Khuriltai — beginning of Mongol-Khanate framework over Inner Mongolian region 1206-1635 + foundation of largest contiguous land empire in world history + foundation framework for subsequent Northern Yuan 1368-1635 + Qing Inner Mongolian league framework.

key_developments.title: Northern Yuan framework + Mongol retreat
key_developments.description: Northern Yuan framework 1368-1635 — Ming Hongwu dynasty conquest of Yuan capital Khanbaliq (Beijing) August 1368 forced Mongol Yuan court retreat to Mongolian steppes — pivotal pre-modern Northern-Yuan-Mongolian framework foundation period bridging Yuan 1271-1368 and subsequent Qing 1635 Inner Mongolian league framework.

key_developments.title: Qing Inner Mongolian league framework
key_developments.description: Qing protectorate over Inner Mongolia established 1635 following Qing conquest of Chakhar Mongol Lighdan Khan + integration of Inner Mongolian bannermen into Qing eight-banners framework — Qing-Mongolian league framework 1635-1912 + foundation for subsequent Republican 1912-1947 + PRC 1947+ frameworks.

key_developments.title: Mongolian People's Republic established
key_developments.description: Mongolian People's Republic established 26 November 1924 in Outer Mongolia (modern Mongolia) — distinct framework from Inner Mongolia — pivotal modern Mongolian-people split-jurisdiction framework foundation + structurally distinctive Mongolian-people Inner-vs-Outer-Mongolia split-jurisdiction framework continuing to present.

key_developments.title: Inner Mongolia Autonomous Government established
key_developments.description: Inner Mongolia Autonomous Government established 1 May 1947 by Ulanhu (Inner Mongolian Communist leader) — preceding founding of People's Republic of China by 2 years 5 months — structurally distinctive globally oldest Chinese autonomous-region in modern Chinese state formation history + precedent template for all subsequent Chinese autonomous-region frameworks.

key_developments.title: PRC incorporation
key_developments.description: Inner Mongolia formally incorporated into People's Republic of China 1 October 1949 — Inner Mongolia retained autonomous-region status as charter member of PRC Regional Ethnic Autonomy framework + Ulanhu retained leadership.

key_developments.title: Cultural Revolution Inner Mongolian purges
key_developments.description: Cultural Revolution Inner Mongolian purges 1968-1969 — 'Internal Mongolian People's Party' (Nei-Ren-Dang) purge campaign targeting Inner Mongolian intellectuals + officials + Mongolian-language educators — ~22,000-200,000 deaths estimated + significant context shaping subsequent post-Mao Inner Mongolian autonomy framework + foundation for subsequent 1980s rehabilitation framework.

key_developments.title: Regional Ethnic Autonomy Law
key_developments.description: Chinese Federal Regional Ethnic Autonomy Law of 31 May 1984 providing legal framework for Inner Mongolia Autonomous Region — pivotal post-Mao modernisation framework + foundation for subsequent 2001 revision framework + cementing Inner Mongolia autonomy framework within PRC constitutional framework.

key_developments.title: Inner Mongolian-language education protests
key_developments.description: Inner Mongolian-language education protests August-September 2020 — protests against Chinese Government policy reducing Mongolian-language instruction in Inner Mongolian schools (gradual transition from Mongolian-medium to Chinese-medium instruction in politics, history, language and literature subjects) — pivotal Mongolian-language-cultural-political-identity contestation framework.

key_developments.title: Continuing Mongolian-language instruction reduction + autonomy framework
key_developments.description: Continuing Inner Mongolia framework 2024 — continuing implementation of 2020 Mongolian-language instruction reduction policy + continuing Inner Mongolia Autonomous Region constitutional autonomy framework + continuing Traditional Mongolian Script ᠮᠣᠩᠭᠣᠯ ᠪᠢᠴᠢᠭ official government usage + continuing Mongolian-people Inner-vs-Outer-Mongolia split-jurisdiction framework + 77 years since 1947 Inner Mongolia Autonomous Government establishment.

key_developments.title: Higher People's Court of Inner Mongolia + Supreme People's Court — best-interests-of-the-child substantive register + Chinese Civil Code 2020 Book V framework
key_developments.description: Higher People's Court of Inner Mongolia (内蒙古自治区高级人民法院) and Supreme People's Court of the PRC continue to develop best-interests-of-the-child jurisprudence under Chinese Civil Code 2020 Book V + Constitution Article 4 regional ethnic autonomy + Regional Ethnic Autonomy Law 1984 framework in custody disputes within continuing Mongolian-language instruction reduction context. Substantive analysis without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label adoption — within Chinese socialist-civil-law framework.

structural_findings: Inner Mongolia operates a federal Chinese civil-law autonomous-region framework — places Inner Mongolia in the Northern Chinese autonomous-region cluster.

Oldest Chinese autonomous-region in modern Chinese state formation history (established 1 May 1947) is structurally distinctive globally — preceding founding of People's Republic of China by 2 years 5 months, precedent template for all subsequent Chinese autonomous-region frameworks.
Third-largest Chinese province-level division by area (~1,183,000 km² / ~12.3% of Chinese land area) is structurally distinctive within Chinese province-level cluster — third after Xinjiang + Tibet.

Only modern Chinese sub-national territory operating Traditional Mongolian Script in formal government usage is structurally distinctive globally — Mongolia itself uses Cyrillic-script Mongolian since 1946 Mongolian script reform.
Central jurisdiction of 2020 Inner Mongolian-language education protests is structurally distinctive globally — pivotal Mongolian-language-cultural-political-identity contestation framework.

Mongol Empire 1206-1368 + Northern Yuan 1368-1635 + Qing Inner Mongolian league framework 1635-1912 historical framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern Chinese sub-national territory with continuous Mongol-Khanate-framework historical lineage.

Distinct Inner Mongolia vs Outer Mongolia (Mongolian People's Republic 1924-1992 + modern Mongolia) sub-national framework is structurally distinctive globally — Mongolian-people split-jurisdiction framework.
editorial_notes: Inner Mongolia Autonomous Region jurisdiction sidecar v1.1 — civil-law Northern Chinese Chinese-autonomous-region (1206 Mongol Empire founded + 1368 Northern Yuan framework + 1635 Qing Inner Mongolian league + 1924 Mongolian People's Republic split-jurisdiction + 1947 Inner Mongolia Autonomous Government established by Ulanhu + 1949 PRC incorporation + 1969 Cultural Revolution Nei-Ren-Dang purges + 1984 Regional Ethnic Autonomy Law + 2020 Mongolian-language education protests + 2024 continuing instruction-reduction policy framework). Oldest Chinese autonomous-region in modern Chinese state formation history globally + third-largest Chinese province-level division by area + only modern Chinese sub-national territory operating Traditional Mongolian Script in formal government usage globally + central jurisdiction of 2020 Mongolian-language education protests + Mongol-Khanate-framework continuous historical lineage + Mongolian-people Inner-vs-Outer-Mongolia split-jurisdiction framework.
PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator.

Joins Northern-Chinese + civil-law + Chinese-autonomous-region cluster + oldest-Chinese-autonomous-region-1947-precedent-template-globally-distinctive + Traditional-Mongolian-Script-formal-government-usage + 2020-Inner-Mongolian-language-education-protests + Mongol-Empire-1206-1368-continuous-Khanate-framework + Inner-vs-Outer-Mongolia-split-jurisdiction + Chinese-non-Hague-1980 clusters within the corpus.
